data.title: BLS data halted due to government shutdown, Chicago Fed predicts steady 4.34% unemployment in September 2025. Private sources warn of weak holiday hiring plans.
data.description: The U.S. government shutdown has halted the release of Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) data, postponing the critical September jobs report. The Chicago Fed has stepped in with a forecast predicting a steady unemployment rate of 4.34% for September 2025, based on 11 job market indicators. Private data sources suggest a weak labor market with low holiday hiring plans, potentially affecting payrolls through the end of the year. Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee emphasized the importance of real-time data amid the shutdown. The analysis shows no change in the unemployment rate from August, with layoffs at 2.10% and hiring rates at 45.22%, both slightly lower than the previous month. The forecast includes a 28.2% probability of no change in the reported rate.

data.title: Cosentino unveils new Éclos® brand with zero crystalline silica, high recycled content, innovative design system. Over 28,000 research hours and 1,500 testing hours by Cosentino R&D.
data.description: A new type of mineral surface introduced by Éclos® features zero crystalline silica, a significant amount of recycled materials, and an innovative layered design system. The creation of this surface involved over 28,000 hours of research and more than 1,500 hours of testing carried out by the Cosentino R&D team.

data.title: Business Reply helps Pusterla 1880 with digital transformation project using Oracle Cloud, impacting global operations.
data.description: In Turin, Italy, the Reply company Business Reply assisted Pusterla 1880, a top player in luxury packaging, in a digital transformation initiative. This project focused on enhancing financial, logistics, and production operations worldwide. With more than 140 years in the industry and growing influence in Europe, Asia, and the Americas, Pusterla 1880 sought standardization and optimization across global operations.
